exercises.name: 1/2 Kneel to High Knee Hop
exercises.alternateName: single leg reverse lunge to hop
exercises.description: Start in a half-kneeling position, drive up to standing, and perform a high knee hop.

exercises.name: 1/2 Kneel to Lateral Bound
exercises.description: From a half-kneeling position, explosively jump sideways into a bound.

exercises.name: 3D Lunge
exercises.description:
1) 3D lunge has 3 separate lunges that you will perform. 

2) First, the forward lunge. Stepping forward, ensuring that your knee doesnât go over top of your toes. Kneeling tall over top of the back leg. 

3) Return. 

4) Second is the side lunge. Step out, ensuring that your foot, knee and hip are all in line.  And sit your back behind you. 

5) Third, the reverse lunge. Step back and return to standing position.

exercises.name: 90-90 Hip Rotations
exercises.description: Start in a 90-90 seated position and lift the back leg to transition to the opposite 90-90 position. Repeat fluidly.

exercises.name: 90-90 hip stretch
exercises.description: Sit with your legs in a 90-degree angle, one leg in front and the other bent to the side. Hold the stretch to open the hips.

exercises.name: 90-90 Hip Stretch (Internal Bias)
exercises.description: Sit with knees at 90 degrees, but emphasize internal hip rotation by leaning slightly toward the back leg.

exercises.name: 90-90 Hip Switch
exercises.description: From a seated 90-90 position, switch legs to the opposite side, focusing on a gentle hip stretch.

exercises.name: 90-90 Hip Switch to Lift and Reach
exercises.alternateName: window wipers to lift and stretch
exercises.description: Transition from a seated 90-90 position to a hip lift, engaging the core as you lift the hips.

exercises.name: 90-90 Hip Windshield Wipers
exercises.description: Sit in a 90-90 position and slowly rotate your knees from side to side like windshield wipers.

exercises.name: 90-90 to Hip Raise
exercises.alternateName: alternating window wipers to glute thrust
exercises.description: From the 90-90 position, drive through your back leg to lift your hips off the floor, engaging your glutes.

exercises.name: Ab Crunch
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, shorten the distance between the top of the pelvis and the bottom of your rib cage while rolling your rib cage up off the mat. 

2) Be sure not to strain your neck by moving your head forward.

exercises.name: Ab dolly roll outs
exercises.description: Keep toes on the ground, core tight and pelvis tucked.

exercises.name: Ab Roller Wheel Abdominal Roll Out
exercises.description:
1) Using the ab roller, start by holding the ab roller in your hands with arms stretched out and your knees touching the floor. 

2) Begin by rolling the ab roller forward until your body is stretched out as far as you can go. 

3) Pause and pull yourself back to the starting position. 

4) Make sure your body does not touch the ground.

exercises.name: Active Adductor Stretch
exercises.description:
Start in a modified kneeling plank with one knee slightly flared outward and the other leg extended laterally. 

Place both hands on the floor in front of you for support. Drive your hips back toward the bent knee, emphasizing a deep stretch along the inner thigh of the extended leg. 

Rock gently back and forth while maintaining a strong, engaged upper body.

exercises.name: Active Leg Lowering to Bolster
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your back on a mat, with a foam roller or a bolster at your feet. 

2. Raise both legs directly up into the air, keeping the knees as straight as possible.

3. While keeping a stiff abdomen, slowly lower one leg down to touch the bolster while keeping the other leg in place.

4. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Active Straight Leg Drop
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with one leg extended straight up and the other hovering above the floor. 

Lower the raised leg slowly while keeping your core engaged, then return to the start. 

Switch legs and repeat.

exercises.name: Adductor Foam Roller
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility release, you want to ensure that you got along the muscle and try not to roll over top of any bony protrusions, or areas.

2. The adductor runs from the inside of your groin on the inside of your leg all the way up to the inner most part of your knee.

3. When you roll, you don't want to get to the bony part of your knee, just roll right up to it - not on it.

4. You'll want to lay your bodyweight on there as much as you can tolerate. Should be painful but not too much.

5. Lie face down with one leg on a foam roll.

6. Rotate the leg so that the foam roll contacts against your inner thigh. Shift as much weight onto the foam roll as can be tolerated.

exercises.name: Adductor Stretch with Thoracic Twist
exercises.description:
Start on all fours in a quadruped position with hands under shoulders and knees under hips. Step one knee out to the side to open up the hip and adductor of that leg while keeping the opposite knee under your hip. 

Place your hands on the floor for support. Rotate your torso toward the extended leg, reaching the arm on the same side of the bent knee upward toward the ceiling, performing a controlled thoracic twist.

exercises.name: Agility Ladder Warm Up
exercises.description: Agility Ladder Warm Up

exercises.name: Agility: 90-Degree Hops
exercises.description: Jump and rotate 90 degrees mid-air before landing, ensuring controlled landings.

exercises.name: Agility: Crossover Steps
exercises.description: Step one foot across the other in a lateral shuffle, improving foot coordination.

exercises.name: Agility: Dissociation Steps
exercises.description:
Step laterally in quick alternating patterns while keeping your hips and upper body facing forward. 

Move your feet rapidly side to side across the mat, maintaining a low athletic stance and controlled torso. 

Repeat continuously for speed and coordination.

exercises.name: Agility: In/Out Hops
exercises.description: Jump in and out with both feet together, mimicking a ladder drill.
